constraints.expression: consumer recomputes receipt_hash via safeCanonicalize(receipt-minus-receipt_hash) and rejects the record on mismatch
constraints.message: receipt_hash MUST equal SHA-256 over safeCanonicalize(receipt-minus-receipt_hash). The library does NOT recompute or compare; consumer-side reconciliation is required.
constraints.evaluation_note: Content-addressed-hash reconciliation is consumer-side per ADR-010. Same shape as RP-1 for RecallPack — see that rule's evaluation_note for the consumer obligation. The receipt_hash commits to the receipt body minus its own receipt_hash field; pack_hash is referenced by value (not recursed) so the receipt's canonical form is independent of the underlying pack body.

constraints.expression: consumer verifies signature_envelope.signature_value against the public key referenced by signature_envelope.key_ref + the SignerEntry it resolves to
constraints.message: signature_envelope.signature_value MUST verify against the public key referenced by signature_envelope.key_ref. The library does NOT verify; consumer-side verification is required (CRYPTO_DEFERRED).
constraints.evaluation_note: Cryptographic signature verification is consumer-side per NF-1 + ADR-010. The schema-level x-crypto-bearing flag plus the validate() safe-by-default branch ensure the library refuses to return valid:true unless the consumer explicitly passes acceptDeferred:true. When opted in, the manifest entry surfaces this obligation under reason: 'crypto_deferred'.
